item.title: Defending champions Argentina stun England 2-1 to book World Cup final clash
item.description: Defending champions Argentina have secured a place in the FIFA World Cup final after coming from behind to beat England 2-1 in a dramatic semi-final. After a goalless first half, Anthony Gordon gave England the lead in the 55th minute following an assist from Morgan Rogers.
item.content:encoded:
Defending champions Argentina have secured a place in the FIFA World Cup final after coming from behind to beat England 2-1 in a dramatic semi-final.

After a goalless first half, Anthony Gordon gave England the lead in the 55th minute following an assist from Morgan Rogers.

England remained ahead until Enzo Fernández equalised for Argentina in the 85th minute, with Lionel Messi providing the assist.

Lautaro Martínez then scored the winning goal in the second minute of added time, again assisted by Messi, to complete Argentina’s late comeback.

The third-place play-off between England and France will be played at 9am on Sunday.

Argentina will now face Spain in the FIFA World Cup final at 7am on Monday.

item.title: No matter how successful I am on the field, family keeps me grounded - Messi
item.description: The 39-year-old, who captained Argentina to the 2022 FIFA World Cup title, says he tries to live a normal life with his wife, Antonela Roccuzzo, and their three sons, Thiago, Matteo and Ciro. Messi says he spends time with his family, disciplines his children and shares the same responsibilities as any other parent.
item.content:encoded:
The 39-year-old, who captained Argentina to the 2022 FIFA World Cup title, says he tries to live a normal life with his wife, Antonela Roccuzzo, and their three sons, Thiago, Matteo and Ciro.

Messi says he spends time with his family, disciplines his children and shares the same responsibilities as any other parent.

He says despite the fame and recognition he receives around the world, his family helps keep him grounded.

Messi and Roccuzzo first met as children in their hometown of Rosario, Argentina, and have been together since their teenage years. They married in 2017 and have three sons together.

item.title: Let's make it uncomfortable for the visiting team with a sellout crowd on Saturday - Legge
item.description: McDonald's Fiji Drua Women coach Mike Legge is calling on fans from around Fiji to pack Churchill Park this Saturday as his side looks to book a place in the Super W final. The Drua Women will host their home semi-final at 1pm in Lautoka after securing a top-two finish, and Legge says the team has earned the right to play in front of a full house.
item.content:encoded:
McDonald's Fiji Drua Women coach Mike Legge is calling on fans from around Fiji to pack Churchill Park this Saturday as his side looks to book a place in the Super W final.

The Drua Women will host their home semi-final at 1pm in Lautoka after securing a top-two finish, and Legge says the team has earned the right to play in front of a full house.

Legge describes hosting the semi-final as a massive milestone for the club but says the focus now is on creating an unforgettable atmosphere.

Insert: Legge - Let's make it uncomfortable for them July 15

The Fiji Drua kick off their Super W semi-final at 1pm this Saturday at Churchill Park in Lautoka, with a place in the grand final on the line.

item.title: Junior Bula Boys thrash Samoa 5-0 to move closer to OFC U-16 semi-finals
item.description: The Junior Bula Boys have taken a big step towards the semi-finals of the OFC U-16 Men's Championship after thrashing Samoa 5-0 in Papua New Guinea. After opening their campaign with a 2-nil win over New Caledonia, Fiji gave another commanding performance.
item.content:encoded:
The Junior Bula Boys have taken a big step towards the semi-finals of the OFC U-16 Men's Championship after thrashing Samoa 5-0 in Papua New Guinea.

After opening their campaign with a 2-nil win over New Caledonia, Fiji gave another commanding performance.

Shahil Prasad broke the deadlock in the 19th minute before Ryan Swami doubled Fiji's lead six minutes later.

Isoa Baselala struck just before half-time and completed his brace in the second half to put the result beyond doubt.

Fiji's fifth goal came through a late own goal by Samoa in the match.

The victory leaves Fiji in a strong position to book a place in the semi-finals.

Fiji's next match will be against Papua New Guinea at 6pm on Saturday.

The semi-finals of the OFC U-16 Men's Championship will be held next Thursday, while the final will be played next Saturday.

item.title: Natabua High School student ready to represent Fiji at first Commonwealth Games
item.description: The Year 13 student of Natabua High School says her preparations have been going well, although she is still working on improving a few areas of her performance before the competition. Lutumailagi, who trains with the Nadi Athletics Club, says one of her biggest challenges has been travelling between Nadi and Suva for training sessions with the rest of the national squad.
item.content:encoded:
The Year 13 student of Natabua High School says her preparations have been going well, although she is still working on improving a few areas of her performance before the competition.

Lutumailagi, who trains with the Nadi Athletics Club, says one of her biggest challenges has been travelling between Nadi and Suva for training sessions with the rest of the national squad.

She says despite the long journeys, she has remained focused on her goal of representing Fiji on one of the biggest sporting stages.

Lutumailagi says fellow athlete Makelesi Bulikiobo and her father have been her biggest inspirations throughout her athletics journey.

The Fiji athlete will compete in the women's 800 metres at the 2026 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow, Scotland.

The 23rd Commonwealth Games will be held in Glasgow from next Friday to 2nd August.

item.title: QVS to defend Lutubula Trophy against RKS in Deans Eastern Zone semi-finals
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 15/07/2026 Defending Lutubula Trophy champions Queen Victoria School (QVS) will face Ratu Kadavulevu School (RKS) in the Vodafone Deans Eastern Zone Secondary Schools Rugby Union U18 semi-finals this weekend.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 15/07/2026

Defending Lutubula Trophy champions Queen Victoria School (QVS) will face Ratu Kadavulevu School (RKS) in the Vodafone Deans Eastern Zone Secondary Schools Rugby Union U18 semi-finals this weekend.

The Lutubula Trophy, once the hallmark of dominance among Eastern Zone rugby powerhouses such as QVS, RKS and Lelean Memorial School, forms part of the qualification pathway for schools aiming to reach the Vodafone Deans competition.

Eastern Zone Secondary Schools Rugby Union Association Secretary Epeli Kalou says if QVS loses to RKS this weekend, a new Lutubula Trophy holder will be crowned in the final. However, if QVS successfully defends the trophy, another challenge will be held during next week's final.

Kalou says Lelean Memorial School is the favourite in the U18 grade, having remained unbeaten throughout the competition with victories over both RKS and QVS.

He adds that it will be an exciting weekend of rugby, with both QVS and RKS determined to avoid dropping into the elimination bracket.

Kalou also says this week's semi-finals will be played at St Vincent College Ground in Natovi.

He says the venue was chosen to help reduce travel expenses for St John's College while also providing a neutral venue for all four semi-finalists.

The U18 semi-finals will see Ratu Kadavulevu School take on Queen Victoria School, while Lelean Memorial School face St John's College.

The Eastern Zone semi finals will be held at St Vincent College Ground and Dawasamu Ground.

item.title: Collision battle and set-piece key to Drua's semi-final hopes against Reds - Legge
item.description: The McDonald's Fiji Drua Women will need to win the collision battle, execute their skills and shut down the Queensland Reds' powerful forward pack if they are to book a place in the Super Rugby Women final this weekend. Head coach Mike Legge says those three areas will be crucial when the Drua host the Reds in their home semi-final at Churchill Park in Lautoka.
item.content:encoded:
The McDonald's Fiji Drua Women will need to win the collision battle, execute their skills and shut down the Queensland Reds' powerful forward pack if they are to book a place in the Super Rugby Women final this weekend.

Head coach Mike Legge says those three areas will be crucial when the Drua host the Reds in their home semi-final at Churchill Park in Lautoka.

Legge says the Reds are a physical side that thrive on winning collisions, and his players will need to match that intensity while maintaining discipline in their execution.

Insert: Legge - Make sure we score points July 15

Legge also highlighted the Reds' forward pack as one of their biggest strengths, pointing to their dominance at set-piece during the teams' round-robin encounter.

He says Queensland rely heavily on their scrum, powerful ball carriers, including Eva Karpani, and a strong line-out.

Legge says neutralising the Reds' scrum and line-out while applying pressure in defence will give the Drua the best chance of progressing to the final.

The Drua will host the Reds in the Super Rugby Women semi-final at Churchill Park in Lautoka on Saturday at 1pm.

The second semi-final will see the Western Force facing the Waratahs at 6.05pm.

item.description: Lekutu Secondary School has created history by qualifying for the semi-finals of the Fiji Secondary Schools competition for the fi

item.title: Spain beat France 2-0 to reach the FIFA World Cup final after 16 years
item.description: After 16 years Spain are back in the FIFA World Cup final after defeating France 2-0 in the first semi-final. Mikel Oyarzabal open

item.title: France seek revenge on Spain to reach World Cup final
item.description: French midfielder Warren Zaïre-Emery says his team wants to exact revenge on Spain and banish painful memories of their exit in th

item.title: Four Marist teams eye Southern Zone rugby finals berths
item.description: Marist Brothers High School will feature in four grades during the Vodafone Deans Southern Zone Secondary School Rugby Union semi-
